#include \"source/server/drain_manager_impl.h\"\n36: #include \"source/common/listener_manager/filter_chain_manager_impl.h\"\n37: #include \"source/server/transport_socket_config_impl.h\"\n38: \n39: namespace Envoy {\n40: namespace Server {\n41: namespace {\n42: \n43: std::string toString(Network::Socket::Type socket_type) {\n44:   switch (socket_type) {\n45:   case Network::Socket::Type::Stream:\n46:     return \"SocketType::Stream\";\n47:   case Network::Socket::Type::Datagram:\n48:     return \"SocketType::Datagram\";\n49:   }\n50:   return \"\";\n51: }\n52: \n53: // Finds and returns the DynamicListener for the name provided from listener_map, creating and\n54: // inserting one if necessary.\n55: envoy::admin::v3::ListenersConfigDump::DynamicListener* getOrCreateDynamicListener(\n56:     const std::string& name, envoy::admin::v3::ListenersConfigDump& dump,\n57:     absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, envoy::admin::v3::ListenersConfigDump::DynamicListener*>&\n58:         listener_map) {\n59: \n60:   auto it = listener_map.find(name);\n61:   if (it != listener_map.end()) {\n62:     return it->second;\n63:   }\n64:   auto* state = dump.add_dynamic_listeners();\n65:   state->set_name(name);\n66:   listener_map.emplace(name, state);\n67:   return state;\n68: }\n69: \n70: // Given a listener, dumps the version info, update time and configuration into the\n71: // DynamicListenerState provided.\n72: void fillState(envoy::admin::v3::ListenersConfigDump::DynamicListenerState& state,\n73:                const ListenerImpl& listener) {\n74:   state.set_version_info(listener.versionInfo());\n75:   state.mutable_listener()->PackFrom(listener.config());\n76:   TimestampUtil::systemClockToTimestamp(listener.last_updated_, *(state.mutable_last_updated()));\n77: }\n78: } // namespace\n79: \n80: absl::StatusOr<Filter::NetworkFilterFactoriesList>\n81: ProdListenerComponentFactory::createNetworkFilterFactoryListImpl(\n82:     const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::Filter>& filters,\n83:     Server::Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContext& filter_chain_factory_context,\n84:     Filter::NetworkFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl& config_provider_manager) {\n85:   Filter::NetworkFilterFactoriesList ret;\n86:   ret.reserve(filters.size());\n87:   for (ssize_t i = 0; i < filters.size(); i++) {\n88:     const auto& proto_config = filters[i];\n89:     const bool is_terminal = i == filters.size() - 1;\n90:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"  filter #{}:\", i);\n91: \n92:     if (proto_config.config_type_case() ==\n93:         envoy::config::listener::v3::Filter::ConfigTypeCase::kConfigDiscovery) {\n94:       ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"      dynamic filter name: {}\", proto_config.name());\n95:       ret.push_back(config_provider_manager.createDynamicFilterConfigProvider(\n96:           proto_config.config_discovery(), proto_config.name(),\n97:           filter_chain_factory_context.serverFactoryContext(), filter_chain_factory_context,\n98:           filter_chain_factory_context.serverFactoryContext().clusterManager(), is_terminal,\n99:           \"network\", nullptr));\n100:       continue;\n101:     }\n102: \n103:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"    name: {}\", proto_config.name());\n104:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"  config: {}\",\n105:               MessageUtil::convertToStringForLogs(\n106:                   static_cast<const Protobuf::Message&>(proto_config.typed_config())));\n107: \n108:     // Now see if there is a factory that will accept the config.\n109:     auto& factory =\n110:         Config::Utility::getAndCheckFactory<Configuration::NamedNetworkFilterConfigFactory>(\n111:             proto_config);\n112: \n113:     auto message = Config::Utility::translateToFactoryConfig(\n114:         proto_config, filter_chain_factory_context.messageValidationVisitor(), factory);\n115:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(Config::Utility::validateTerminalFilters(\n116:         filters[i].name(), factory.name(), \"network\",\n117:         factory.isTerminalFilterByProto(*message,\n118:                                         filter_chain_factory_context.serverFactoryContext()),\n119:         is_terminal));\n120:     auto callback_or_error =\n121:         factory.createFilterFactoryFromProto(*message, filter_chain_factory_context);\n122:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(callback_or_error.status());\n123:     ret.push_back(config_provider_manager.createStaticFilterConfigProvider(*callback_or_error,\n124:                                                                            proto_config.name()));\n125:   }\n126:   return ret;\n127: }\n128: \n129: absl::StatusOr<Filter::ListenerFilterFactoriesList>\n130: ProdListenerComponentFactory::createListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(\n131:     const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n132:     Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context,\n133:     Filter::TcpListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl& config_provider_manager) {\n134:   Filter::ListenerFilterFactoriesList ret;\n135: \n136:   ret.reserve(filters.size());\n137:   for (ssize_t i = 0; i < filters.size(); i++) {\n138:     const auto& proto_config = filters[i];\n139:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"  filter #{}:\", i);\n140:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"    name: {}\", proto_config.name());\n141:     // dynamic listener filter configuration\n142:     if (proto_config.config_type_case() ==\n143:         envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter::ConfigTypeCase::kConfigDiscovery) {\n144:       const auto& config_discovery = proto_config.config_discovery();\n145:       const auto& name = proto_config.name();\n146:       if (config_discovery.apply_default_config_without_warming() &&\n147:           !config_discovery.has_default_config()) {\n148:         return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n149:             \"Error: listener filter config {} applied without warming but has no default config.\",\n150:             name));\n151:       }\n152:       for (const auto& type_url : config_discovery.type_urls()) {\n153:         const auto factory_type_url = TypeUtil::typeUrlToDescriptorFullName(type_url);\n154:         const auto* factory =\n155:             Registry::FactoryRegistry<Server::Configuration::NamedListenerFilterConfigFactory>::\n156:                 getFactoryByType(factory_type_url);\n157:         if (factory == nullptr) {\n158:           return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n159:               \"Error: no listener factory found for a required type URL {}.\", factory_type_url));\n160:         }\n161:       }\n162:       auto filter_config_provider = config_provider_manager.createDynamicFilterConfigProvider(\n163:           config_discovery, name, context.serverFactoryContext(), context,\n164:           context.serverFactoryContext().clusterManager(), false, \"tcp-listener\",\n165:           createListenerFilterMatcher(proto_config));\n166:       ret.push_back(std::move(filter_config_provider));\n167:     } else {\n168:       ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"  config: {}\",\n169:                 MessageUtil::convertToStringForLogs(\n170:                     static_cast<const Protobuf::Message&>(proto_config.typed_config())));\n171:       // For static configuration, now see if there is a factory that will accept the config.\n172:       auto& factory =\n173:           Config::Utility::getAndCheckFactory<Configuration::NamedListenerFilterConfigFactory>(\n174:               proto_config);\n175:       const auto message = Config::Utility::translateToFactoryConfig(\n176:           proto_config, context.messageValidationVisitor(), factory);\n177:       const auto callback = factory.createListenerFilterFactoryFromProto(\n178:           *message, createListenerFilterMatcher(proto_config), context);\n179:       auto filter_config_provider =\n180:           config_provider_manager.createStaticFilterConfigProvider(callback, proto_config.name());\n181:       ret.push_back(std::move(filter_config_provider));\n182:     }\n183:   }\n184:   return ret;\n185: }\n186: \n187: absl::StatusOr<std::vector<Network::UdpListenerFilterFactoryCb>>\n188: ProdListenerComponentFactory::createUdpListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(\n189:     const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n190:     Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context) {\n191:   std::vector<Network::UdpListenerFilterFactoryCb> ret;\n192:   for (ssize_t i = 0; i < filters.size(); i++) {\n193:     const auto& proto_config = filters[i];\n194:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"  filter #{}:\", i);\n195:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"    name: {}\", proto_config.name());\n196:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"  config: {}\",\n197:               MessageUtil::convertToStringForLogs(\n198:                   static_cast<const Protobuf::Message&>(proto_config.typed_config())));\n199:     if (proto_config.config_type_case() ==\n200:         envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter::ConfigTypeCase::kConfigDiscovery) {\n201:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\"UDP listener filter: {} is configured with \"\n202:                                                     \"unsupported dynamic configuration\",\n203:                                                     proto_config.name()));\n204:       return ret;\n205:     }\n206:     // Now see if there is a factory that will accept the config.\n207:     auto& factory =\n208:         Config::Utility::getAndCheckFactory<Configuration::NamedUdpListenerFilterConfigFactory>(\n209:             proto_config);\n210: \n211:     auto message = Config::Utility::translateToFactoryConfig(\n212:         proto_config, context.messageValidationVisitor(), factory);\n213:     ret.push_back(factory.createFilterFactoryFromProto(*message, context));\n214:   }\n215:   return ret;\n216: }\n217: \n218: absl::StatusOr<Filter::QuicListenerFilterFactoriesList>\n219: ProdListenerComponentFactory::createQuicListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(\n220:     const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\n221:     Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context,\n222:     Filter::QuicListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl& config_provider_manager) {\n223:   Filter::QuicListenerFilterFactoriesList ret;\n224: \n225:   ret.reserve(filters.size());\n226:   for (ssize_t i = 0; i < filters.size(); i++) {\n227:     const auto& proto_config = filters[i];\n228:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"  filter #{}:\", i);\n229:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"    name: {}\", proto_config.name());\n230:     // dynamic listener filter configuration\n231:     if (proto_config.config_type_case() ==\n232:         envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter::ConfigTypeCase::kConfigDiscovery) {\n233:       const envoy::config::core::v3::ExtensionConfigSource& config_discovery =\n234:           proto_config.config_discovery();\n235:       const std::string& name = proto_config.name();\n236:       if (config_discovery.apply_default_config_without_warming() &&\n237:           !config_discovery.has_default_config()) {\n238:         return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n239:             \"Error: listener filter config {} applied without warming but has no default config.\",\n240:             name));\n241:       }\n242:       for (absl::string_view type_url : config_discovery.type_urls()) {\n243:         absl::string_view factory_type_url = TypeUtil::typeUrlToDescriptorFullName(type_url);\n244:         if (Registry::FactoryRegistry<Server::Configuration::NamedQuicListenerFilterConfigFactory>::\n245:                 getFactoryByType(factory_type_url) == nullptr) {\n246:           return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n247:               \"Error: no listener factory found for a required type URL {}.\", factory_type_url));\n248:         }\n249:       }\n250:       ret.push_back(config_provider_manager.createDynamicFilterConfigProvider(\n251:           config_discovery, name, context.serverFactoryContext(), context,\n252:           context.serverFactoryContext().clusterManager(), false, \"quic-listener\",\n253:           createListenerFilterMatcher(proto_config)));\n254:     } else {\n255:       ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"  config: {}\",\n256:                 MessageUtil::convertToStringForLogs(\n257:                     static_cast<const Protobuf::Message&>(proto_config.typed_config())));\n258:       // For static configuration, now see if there is a factory that will accept the config.\n259:       auto& factory =\n260:           Config::Utility::getAndCheckFactory<Configuration::NamedQuicListenerFilterConfigFactory>(\n261:               proto_config);\n262:       const auto message = Config::Utility::translateToFactoryConfig(\n263:           proto_config, context.messageValidationVisitor(), factory);\n264:       const auto callback = factory.createListenerFilterFactoryFromProto(\n265:           *message, createListenerFilterMatcher(proto_config), context);\n266:       ret.push_back(\n267:           config_provider_manager.createStaticFilterConfigProvider(callback, proto_config.name()));\n268:     }\n269:   }\n270:   return ret;\n271: }\n272: \n273: Network::ListenerFilterMatcherSharedPtr ProdListenerComponentFactory::createListenerFilterMatcher(\n274:     const envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter& listener_filter) {\n275:   if (!listener_filter.has_filter_disabled()) {\n276:     return nullptr;\n277:   }\n278:   return {Network::ListenerFilterMatcherBuilder::buildListenerFilterMatcher(\n279:       listener_filter.filter_disabled())};\n280: }\n281: \n282: absl::StatusOr<Network::SocketSharedPtr> ProdListenerComponentFactory::createListenSocket(\n283:     Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr address, Network::Socket::Type socket_type,\n284:     const Network::Socket::OptionsSharedPtr& options, BindType bind_type,\n285:     const Network::SocketCreationOptions& creation_options, uint32_t worker_index) {\n286:   ASSERT(socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream ||\n287:          socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Datagram);\n288: \n289:   // First we try to get the socket from our parent if applicable in each case below.\n290:   if (address->type() == Network::Address::Type::Pipe) {\n291:     if (socket_type != Network::Socket::Type::Stream) {\n292:       // This could be implemented in the future, since Unix domain sockets\n293:       // support SOCK_DGRAM, but there would need to be a way to specify it in\n294:       // envoy.api.v2.core.Pipe.\n295:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n296:           fmt::format(\"socket type {} not supported for pipes\", toString(socket_type)));\n297:     }\n298:     const std::string addr = fmt::format(\"unix://{}\", address->asString());\n299:     const int fd = server_.hotRestart().duplicateParentListenSocket(addr, worker_index);\n300:     Network::IoHandlePtr io_handle = std::make_unique<Network::IoSocketHandleImpl>(fd);\n301:     if (io_handle->isOpen()) {\n302:       ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"obtained socket for address {} from parent\", addr);\n303:       return std::make_shared<Network::UdsListenSocket>(std::move(io_handle), address);\n304:     }\n305:     return std::make_shared<Network::UdsListenSocket>(address);\n306:   } else if (address->type() == Network::Address::Type::EnvoyInternal) {\n307:     // Listener manager should have validated that envoy internal address doesn't work with udp\n308:     // listener yet.\n309:     ASSERT(socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream);\n310:     return std::make_shared<Network::InternalListenSocket>(address);\n311:   }\n312: \n313:   const std::string scheme = (socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream)\n314:                                  ? std::string(Network::Utility::TCP_SCHEME)\n315:                                  : std::string(Network::Utility::UDP_SCHEME);\n316:   const std::string addr = absl::StrCat(scheme, address->asString());\n317: \n318:   if (bind_type != BindType::NoBind) {\n319:     const int fd = server_.hotRestart().duplicateParentListenSocket(addr, worker_index);\n320:     if (fd != -1) {\n321:       ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"obtained socket for address {} from parent\", addr);\n322:       Network::IoHandlePtr io_handle = std::make_unique<Network::IoSocketHandleImpl>(fd);\n323:       if (socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream) {\n324:         return std::make_shared<Network::TcpListenSocket>(std::move(io_handle), address, options);\n325:       } else {\n326:         auto socket = std::make_shared<Network::UdpListenSocket>(\n327:             std::move(io_handle), address, options,\n328:             server_.hotRestart().parentDrainedCallbackRegistrar());\n329:         return socket;\n330:       }\n331:     }\n332:   }\n333: \n334:   if (socket_type == Network::Socket::Type::Stream) {\n335:     return std::make_shared<Network::TcpListenSocket>(\n336:         address, options, bind_type != BindType::NoBind, creation_options);\n337:   } else {\n338:     return std::make_shared<Network::UdpListenSocket>(\n339:         address, options, bind_type != BindType::NoBind, creation_options);\n340:   }\n341: }\n342: \n343: DrainManagerPtr ProdListenerComponentFactory::createDrainManager(\n344:     envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener::DrainType drain_type) {\n345:   return DrainManagerPtr{new DrainManagerImpl(server_, drain_type)};\n346: }\n347: \n348: DrainingFilterChainsManager::DrainingFilterChainsManager(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener,\n349:                                                          uint64_t workers_pending_removal)\n350:     : draining_listener_(std::move(draining_listener)),\n351:       workers_pending_removal_(workers_pending_removal) {}\n352: \n353: ListenerManagerImpl::ListenerManagerImpl(Instance& server,\n354:                                          std::unique_ptr<ListenerComponentFactory>&& factory,\n355:                                          WorkerFactory& worker_factory,\n356:                                          bool enable_dispatcher_stats,\n357:                                          Quic::QuicStatNames& quic_stat_names)\n358:     : server_(server), factory_(std::move(factory)),\n359:       scope_(server.stats().createScope(\"listener_manager.\")), stats_(generateStats(*scope_)),\n360:       enable_dispatcher_stats_(enable_dispatcher_stats), quic_stat_names_(quic_stat_names) {\n361:   if (!factory_) {\n362:     factory_ = std::make_unique<ProdListenerComponentFactory>(server);\n363:   }\n364:   if (server.admin().has_value()) {\n365:     config_tracker_entry_ = server.admin()->getConfigTracker().add(\n366:         \"listeners\", [this](const Matchers::StringMatcher& name_matcher) {\n367:           return dumpListenerConfigs(name_matcher);\n368:         });\n369:   }\n370: \n371:   for (uint32_t i = 0; i < server.options().concurrency(); i++) {\n372:     workers_.emplace_back(worker_factory.createWorker(\n373:         i, server.overloadManager(), server.nullOverloadManager(), absl::StrCat(\"worker_\", i)));\n374:   }\n375: }\n376: \n377: ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr\n378: ListenerManagerImpl::dumpListenerConfigs(const Matchers::StringMatcher& name_matcher) {\n379:   auto config_dump = std::make_unique<envoy::admin::v3::ListenersConfigDump>();\n380:   config_dump->set_version_info(lds_api_ != nullptr ? lds_api_->versionInfo() : \"\");\n381: \n382:   using DynamicListener = envoy::admin::v3::ListenersConfigDump::DynamicListener;\n383:   using DynamicListenerState = envoy::admin::v3::ListenersConfigDump::DynamicListenerState;\n384:   absl::flat_hash_map<std::string, DynamicListener*> listener_map;\n385: \n386:   for (const auto& listener : active_listeners_) {\n387:     if (!name_matcher.match(listener->config().name())) {\n388:       continue;\n389:     }\n390:     if (listener->blockRemove()) {\n391:       auto& static_listener = *config_dump->mutable_static_listeners()->Add();\n392:       static_listener.mutable_listener()->PackFrom(listener->config());\n393:       TimestampUtil::systemClockToTimestamp(listener->last_updated_,\n394:                                             *(static_listener.mutable_last_updated()));\n395:       continue;\n396:     }\n397:     // Listeners are always added to active_listeners_ list before workers are started.\n398:     // This applies even when the listeners are still waiting for initialization.\n399:     // To avoid confusion in config dump, in that case, we add these listeners to warming\n400:     // listeners config dump rather than active ones.\n401:     DynamicListener* dynamic_listener =\n402:         getOrCreateDynamicListener(listener->name(), *config_dump, listener_map);\n403: \n404:     DynamicListenerState* dump_listener;\n405:     if (workers_started_) {\n406:       dump_listener = dynamic_listener->mutable_active_state();\n407:     } else {\n408:       dump_listener = dynamic_listener->mutable_warming_state();\n409:     }\n410:     fillState(*dump_listener, *listener);\n411:   }\n412: \n413:   for (const auto& listener : warming_listeners_) {\n414:     if (!name_matcher.match(listener->config().name())) {\n415:       continue;\n416:     }\n417:     DynamicListener* dynamic_listener =\n418:         getOrCreateDynamicListener(listener->name(), *config_dump, listener_map);\n419:     DynamicListenerState* dump_listener = dynamic_listener->mutable_warming_state();\n420:     fillState(*dump_listener, *listener);\n421:   }\n422: \n423:   for (const auto& draining_listener : draining_listeners_) {\n424:     if (!name_matcher.match(draining_listener.listener_->config().name())) {\n425:       continue;\n426:     }\n427:     const auto& listener = draining_listener.listener_;\n428:     DynamicListener* dynamic_listener =\n429:         getOrCreateDynamicListener(listener->name(), *config_dump, listener_map);\n430:     DynamicListenerState* dump_listener = dynamic_listener->mutable_draining_state();\n431:     fillState(*dump_listener, *listener);\n432:   }\n433: \n434:   for (const auto& [error_name, error_state] : error_state_tracker_) {\n435:     DynamicListener* dynamic_listener =\n436:         getOrCreateDynamicListener(error_name, *config_dump, listener_map);\n437: \n438:     const envoy::admin::v3::UpdateFailureState& state = *error_state;\n439:     dynamic_listener->mutable_error_state()->CopyFrom(state);\n440:   }\n441: \n442:   // Dump errors not associated with named listeners.\n443:   for (const auto& error : overall_error_state_) {\n444:     config_dump->add_dynamic_listeners()->mutable_error_state()->CopyFrom(*error);\n445:   }\n446: \n447:   return config_dump;\n448: }\n449: \n450: ListenerManagerStats ListenerManagerImpl::generateStats(Stats::Scope& scope) {\n451:   return {ALL_LISTENER_MANAGER_STATS(POOL_COUNTER(scope), POOL_GAUGE(scope))};\n452: }\n453: \n454: absl::StatusOr<bool>\n455: ListenerManagerImpl::addOrUpdateListener(const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config,\n456:                                          const std::string& version_info, bool added_via_api) {\n457:   std::string name;\n458:   if (!config.name().empty()) {\n459:     name = config.name();\n460:   } else {\n461:     // TODO (soulxu): The random uuid name is bad for logging. We can use listening addresses in\n462:     // the log to improve that.\n463:     name = server_.api().randomGenerator().uuid();\n464:   }\n465: \n466:   // TODO(junr03): currently only one ApiListener can be installed via bootstrap to avoid having to\n467:   // build a collection of listeners, and to have to be able to warm and drain the listeners. In the\n468:   // future allow multiple ApiListeners, and allow them to be created via LDS as well as bootstrap.\n469:   if (config.has_api_listener()) {\n470:     if (config.has_internal_listener()) {\n471:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n472:           \"error adding listener named '{}': api_listener and internal_listener cannot be both set\",\n473:           name));\n474:     }\n475:     if (!api_listener_ && !added_via_api) {\n476:       auto listener_or_error = HttpApiListener::create(config, server_, config.name());\n477:       RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(listener_or_error.status());\n478:       api_listener_ = std::move(listener_or_error.value());\n479:       return true;\n480:     } else {\n481:       ENVOY_LOG(warn, \"listener {} can not be added because currently only one ApiListener is \"\n482:                       \"allowed, and it can only be added via bootstrap configuration\");\n483:       return false;\n484:     }\n485:   }\n486: \n487:   // Address field is not required for internal listeners.\n488:   if (!config.has_internal_listener() && !config.has_address()) {\n489:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n490:         fmt::format(\"error adding listener named '{}': address is necessary\", name));\n491:   }\n492: \n493:   auto it = error_state_tracker_.find(name);\n494:   absl::StatusOr<bool> add_or_update_status;\n495:   TRY_ASSERT_MAIN_THREAD {\n496:     add_or_update_status = addOrUpdateListenerInternal(config, version_info, added_via_api, name);\n497:   }\n498:   END_TRY\n499:   CATCH(const EnvoyException& e, { add_or_update_status = absl::InvalidArgumentError(e.what()); })\n500:   if (!add_or_update_status.status().ok()) {\n501:     if (it == error_state_tracker_.end()) {\n502:       it = error_state_tracker_.emplace(name, std::make_unique<UpdateFailureState>()).first;\n503:     }\n504:     TimestampUtil::systemClockToTimestamp(server_.api().timeSource().systemTime(),\n505:                                           *(it->second->mutable_last_update_attempt()));\n506:     it->second->set_details(add_or_update_status.status().message());\n507:     it->second->mutable_failed_configuration()->PackFrom(config);\n508:   }\n509:   return add_or_update_status;\n510: }\n511: \n512: absl::Status\n513: ListenerManagerImpl::setupSocketFactoryForListener(ListenerImpl& new_listener,\n514:                                                    const ListenerImpl& existing_listener) {\n515:   bool same_socket_options = true;\n516:   if (new_listener.reusePort() != existing_listener.reusePort()) {\n517:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n518:         \"Listener {}: reuse port cannot be changed during an update\", new_listener.name()));\n519:   }\n520: \n521:   same_socket_options = existing_listener.socketOptionsEqual(new_listener);\n522:   if (!same_socket_options && new_listener.reusePort() == false) {\n523:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n524:         fmt::format(\"Listener {}: doesn't support update any socket options \"\n525:                     \"when the reuse port isn't enabled\",\n526:                     new_listener.name()));\n527:   }\n528: \n529:   if (!(existing_listener.hasCompatibleAddress(new_listener) && same_socket_options)) {\n530:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(new_listener.name(), new_listener));\n531:   } else {\n532:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(new_listener.cloneSocketFactoryFrom(existing_listener));\n533:   }\n534:   return absl::OkStatus();\n535: }\n536: \n537: absl::StatusOr<bool> ListenerManagerImpl::addOrUpdateListenerInternal(\n538:     const envoy::config::listener::v3::Listener& config, const std::string& version_info,\n539:     bool added_via_api, const std::string& name) {\n540: \n541:   if (listenersStopped(config)) {\n542:     ENVOY_LOG(\n543:         debug,\n544:         \"listener {} can not be added because listeners in the traffic direction {} are stopped\",\n545:         name, envoy::config::core::v3::TrafficDirection_Name(config.traffic_direction()));\n546:     return false;\n547:   }\n548: \n549:   const uint64_t hash = MessageUtil::hash(config);\n550:   ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"begin add/update listener: name={} hash={}\", name, hash);\n551: \n552:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, name);\n553:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, name);\n554: \n555:   // The listener should be updated back to its original state and the warming listener should be\n556:   // removed.\n557:   if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end() &&\n558:       existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end() &&\n559:       (*existing_active_listener)->blockUpdate(hash)) {\n560:     warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n561:     updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n562:     stats_.listener_modified_.inc();\n563:     return true;\n564:   }\n565: \n566:   // Do a quick blocked update check before going further. This check needs to be done against both\n567:   // warming and active.\n568:   if ((existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end() &&\n569:        (*existing_warming_listener)->blockUpdate(hash)) ||\n570:       (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end() &&\n571:        (*existing_active_listener)->blockUpdate(hash))) {\n572:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"duplicate/locked listener '{}'. no add/update\", name);\n573:     return false;\n574:   }\n575: \n576:   ListenerImplPtr new_listener = nullptr;\n577: \n578:   // In place filter chain update depends on the active listener at worker.\n579:   if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end() &&\n580:       (*existing_active_listener)->supportUpdateFilterChain(config, workers_started_)) {\n581:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"use in place update filter chain update path for listener name={} hash={}\",\n582:               name, hash);\n583:     auto listener_or_error =\n584:         (*existing_active_listener)->newListenerWithFilterChain(config, workers_started_, hash);\n585:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(listener_or_error.status());\n586:     new_listener = std::move(*listener_or_error);\n587:     stats_.listener_in_place_updated_.inc();\n588:   } else {\n589:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"use full listener update path for listener name={} hash={}\", name, hash);\n590:     auto listener_or_error = ListenerImpl::create(config, version_info, *this, name, added_via_api,\n591:                                                   workers_started_, hash);\n592:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(listener_or_error.status());\n593:     new_listener = std::move(*listener_or_error);\n594:   }\n595: \n596:   ListenerImpl& new_listener_ref = *new_listener;\n597: \n598:   bool added = false;\n599:   if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end()) {\n600:     ASSERT(workers_started_);\n601:     new_listener->debugLog(\"update warming listener\");\n602:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setupSocketFactoryForListener(*new_listener, **existing_warming_listener));\n603:     // In this case we can just replace inline.\n604:     *existing_warming_listener = std::move(new_listener);\n605:   } else if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\n606:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setupSocketFactoryForListener(*new_listener, **existing_active_listener));\n607:     // In this case we have no warming listener, so what we do depends on whether workers\n608:     // have been started or not.\n609:     if (workers_started_) {\n610:       new_listener->debugLog(\"add warming listener\");\n611:       warming_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\n612:     } else {\n613:       new_listener->debugLog(\"update active listener\");\n614:       *existing_active_listener = std::move(new_listener);\n615:     }\n616:   } else {\n617:     // We have no warming or active listener so we need to make a new one. What we do depends on\n618:     // whether workers have been started or not.\n619:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(name, *new_listener));\n620:     if (workers_started_) {\n621:       new_listener->debugLog(\"add warming listener\");\n622:       warming_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\n623:     } else {\n624:       new_listener->debugLog(\"add active listener\");\n625:       active_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(new_listener));\n626:     }\n627: \n628:     added = true;\n629:   }\n630: \n631:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n632:   if (added) {\n633:     stats_.listener_added_.inc();\n634:   } else {\n635:     stats_.listener_modified_.inc();\n636:   }\n637: \n638:   new_listener_ref.initialize();\n639:   return true;\n640: }\n641: \n642: bool ListenerManagerImpl::hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(const ListenerList& list,\n643:                                                            const ListenerImpl& listener) {\n644:   for (const auto& existing_listener : list) {\n645:     if (existing_listener->hasDuplicatedAddress(listener)) {\n646:       return true;\n647:     }\n648:   }\n649:   return false;\n650: }\n651: \n652: void ListenerManagerImpl::drainListener(ListenerImplPtr&& listener) {\n653:   // First add the listener to the draining list.\n654:   std::list<DrainingListener>::iterator draining_it = draining_listeners_.emplace(\n655:       draining_listeners_.begin(), std::move(listener), workers_.size());\n656: \n657:   // Using set() avoids a multiple modifiers problem during the multiple processes phase of hot\n658:   // restart. Same below inside the lambda.\n659:   stats_.total_listeners_draining_.set(draining_listeners_.size());\n660: \n661:   // Tell all workers to stop accepting new connections on this listener.\n662:   draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"draining listener\");\n663:   const uint64_t listener_tag = draining_it->listener_->listenerTag();\n664:   stopListener(*draining_it->listener_, {}, [this, listener_tag]() {\n665:     for (auto& listener : draining_listeners_) {\n666:       if (listener.listener_->listenerTag() == listener_tag) {\n667:         maybeCloseSocketsForListener(*listener.listener_);\n668:       }\n669:     }\n670:   });\n671: \n672:   // Start the drain sequence which completes when the listener's drain manager has completed\n673:   // draining at whatever the server configured drain times are.\n674:   draining_it->listener_->localDrainManager().startDrainSequence([this, draining_it]() -> void {\n675:     draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"removing draining listener\");\n676:     for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n677:       // Once the drain time has completed via the drain manager's timer, we tell the workers\n678:       // to remove the listener.\n679:       worker->removeListener(*draining_it->listener_, [this, draining_it]() -> void {\n680:         // The remove listener completion is called on the worker thread. We post back to the\n681:         // main thread to avoid locking. This makes sure that we don't destroy the listener\n682:         // while filters might still be using its context (stats, etc.).\n683:         server_.dispatcher().post([this, draining_it]() -> void {\n684:           if (--draining_it->workers_pending_removal_ == 0) {\n685:             draining_it->listener_->debugLog(\"draining listener removal complete\");\n686:             draining_listeners_.erase(draining_it);\n687:             stats_.total_listeners_draining_.set(draining_listeners_.size());\n688:           }\n689:         });\n690:       });\n691:     }\n692:   });\n693: \n694:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n695: }\n696: \n697: ListenerManagerImpl::ListenerList::iterator\n698: ListenerManagerImpl::getListenerByName(ListenerList& listeners, const std::string& name) {\n699:   auto ret = listeners.end();\n700:   for (auto it = listeners.begin(); it != listeners.end(); ++it) {\n701:     if ((*it)->name() == name) {\n702:       // There should only ever be a single listener per name in the list. We could return faster\n703:       // but take the opportunity to assert that fact.\n704:       ASSERT(ret == listeners.end());\n705:       ret = it;\n706:     }\n707:   }\n708:   return ret;\n709: }\n710: \n711: std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>>\n712: ListenerManagerImpl::listeners(ListenerState state) {\n713:   std::vector<std::reference_wrapper<Network::ListenerConfig>> ret;\n714: \n715:   size_t size = 0;\n716:   size += state & WARMING ? warming_listeners_.size() : 0;\n717:   size += state & ACTIVE ? active_listeners_.size() : 0;\n718:   size += state & DRAINING ? draining_listeners_.size() : 0;\n719:   ret.reserve(size);\n720: \n721:   if (state & WARMING) {\n722:     for (const auto& listener : warming_listeners_) {\n723:       ret.push_back(*listener);\n724:     }\n725:   }\n726:   if (state & ACTIVE) {\n727:     for (const auto& listener : active_listeners_) {\n728:       ret.push_back(*listener);\n729:     }\n730:   }\n731:   if (state & DRAINING) {\n732:     for (const auto& draining_listener : draining_listeners_) {\n733:       ret.push_back(*(draining_listener.listener_));\n734:     }\n735:   }\n736:   return ret;\n737: }\n738: \n739: bool ListenerManagerImpl::doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n740:   TRY_ASSERT_MAIN_THREAD {\n741:     for (auto& socket_factory : listener.listenSocketFactories()) {\n742:       absl::Status success = (socket_factory->doFinalPreWorkerInit());\n743:       if (!success.ok()) {\n744:         ENVOY_LOG(error, \"final pre-worker listener init for listener '{}' failed: {}\",\n745:                   listener.name(), success.message());\n746:         return false;\n747:       }\n748:     }\n749:     return true;\n750:   }\n751:   END_TRY\n752:   CATCH(EnvoyException & e, {\n753:     ENVOY_LOG(error, \"final pre-worker listener init for listener '{}' failed: {}\", listener.name(),\n754:               e.what());\n755:     return false;\n756:   });\n757: }\n758: \n759: void ListenerManagerImpl::addListenerToWorker(Worker& worker,\n760:                                               absl::optional<uint64_t> overridden_listener,\n761:                                               ListenerImpl& listener,\n762:                                               ListenerCompletionCallback completion_callback) {\n763:   if (overridden_listener.has_value()) {\n764:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"replacing existing listener {}\", overridden_listener.value());\n765:   }\n766:   worker.addListener(\n767:       overridden_listener, listener,\n768:       [this, completion_callback]() -> void {\n769:         // The add listener completion runs on the worker thread. Post back to the main thread to\n770:         // avoid locking.\n771:         server_.dispatcher().post([this, completion_callback]() -> void {\n772:           stats_.listener_create_success_.inc();\n773:           if (completion_callback) {\n774:             completion_callback();\n775:           }\n776:         });\n777:       },\n778:       server_.runtime(), server_.api().randomGenerator());\n779: }\n780: \n781: void ListenerManagerImpl::onListenerWarmed(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n782:   // The warmed listener should be added first so that the worker will accept new connections\n783:   // when it stops listening on the old listener.\n784:   if (!doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(listener)) {\n785:     incListenerCreateFailureStat();\n786:     // TODO(mattklein123): Technically we don't need to remove the active listener if one exists.\n787:     // The following call will remove both.\n788:     removeListenerInternal(listener.name(), true);\n789:     return;\n790:   }\n791:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n792:     addListenerToWorker(*worker, absl::nullopt, listener, nullptr);\n793:   }\n794: \n795:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, listener.name());\n796:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, listener.name());\n797: \n798:   (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"warm complete. updating active listener\");\n799:   if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\n800:     // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainListener` as it depends on their\n801:     // state.\n802:     auto listener = std::move(*existing_active_listener);\n803:     *existing_active_listener = std::move(*existing_warming_listener);\n804:     drainListener(std::move(listener));\n805:   } else {\n806:     active_listeners_.emplace_back(std::move(*existing_warming_listener));\n807:   }\n808: \n809:   warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n810:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n811: }\n812: \n813: void ListenerManagerImpl::inPlaceFilterChainUpdate(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n814:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, listener.name());\n815:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, listener.name());\n816:   ASSERT(existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end());\n817:   ASSERT(*existing_warming_listener != nullptr);\n818: \n819:   (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"execute in place filter chain update\");\n820: \n821:   // Now that in place filter chain update was decided, the replaced listener must be in active\n822:   // list. It requires stop/remove listener procedure cancelling the in placed update if any.\n823:   ASSERT(existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end());\n824:   ASSERT(*existing_active_listener != nullptr);\n825: \n826:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n827:     // Explicitly override the existing listener with a new listener config.\n828:     addListenerToWorker(*worker, listener.listenerTag(), listener, nullptr);\n829:   }\n830: \n831:   auto previous_listener = std::move(*existing_active_listener);\n832:   *existing_active_listener = std::move(*existing_warming_listener);\n833:   // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainFilterChains` as it depends on\n834:   // their state.\n835:   drainFilterChains(std::move(previous_listener), **existing_active_listener);\n836: \n837:   warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n838:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n839: }\n840: \n841: void ListenerManagerImpl::drainFilterChains(ListenerImplPtr&& draining_listener,\n842:                                             ListenerImpl& new_listener) {\n843:   // First add the listener to the draining list.\n844:   std::list<DrainingFilterChainsManager>::iterator draining_group =\n845:       draining_filter_chains_manager_.emplace(draining_filter_chains_manager_.begin(),\n846:                                               std::move(draining_listener), workers_.size());\n847:   draining_group->getDrainingListener().diffFilterChain(\n848:       new_listener, [&draining_group](Network::DrainableFilterChain& filter_chain) mutable {\n849:         filter_chain.startDraining();\n850:         draining_group->addFilterChainToDrain(filter_chain);\n851:       });\n852:   auto filter_chain_size = draining_group->numDrainingFilterChains();\n853:   stats_.total_filter_chains_draining_.add(filter_chain_size);\n854:   draining_group->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\n855:       absl::StrCat(\"draining \", filter_chain_size, \" filter chains in listener \",\n856:                    draining_group->getDrainingListener().name()));\n857: \n858:   // Start the drain sequence which completes when the listener's drain manager has completed\n859:   // draining at whatever the server configured drain times are.\n860:   draining_group->startDrainSequence(\n861:       server_.options().drainTime(), server_.dispatcher(), [this, draining_group]() -> void {\n862:         draining_group->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\n863:             absl::StrCat(\"removing draining filter chains from listener \",\n864:                          draining_group->getDrainingListener().name()));\n865:         for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n866:           // Once the drain time has completed via the drain manager's timer, we tell the workers\n867:           // to remove the filter chains.\n868:           worker->removeFilterChains(\n869:               draining_group->getDrainingListenerTag(), draining_group->getDrainingFilterChains(),\n870:               [this, draining_group]() -> void {\n871:                 // The remove listener completion is called on the worker thread. We post back to\n872:                 // the main thread to avoid locking. This makes sure that we don't destroy the\n873:                 // listener while filters might still be using its context (stats, etc.).\n874:                 server_.dispatcher().post([this, draining_group]() -> void {\n875:                   if (draining_group->decWorkersPendingRemoval() == 0) {\n876:                     draining_group->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\n877:                         absl::StrCat(\"draining filter chains from listener \",\n878:                                      draining_group->getDrainingListener().name(), \" complete\"));\n879:                     stats_.total_filter_chains_draining_.sub(\n880:                         draining_group->numDrainingFilterChains());\n881:                     draining_filter_chains_manager_.erase(draining_group);\n882:                   }\n883:                 });\n884:               });\n885:         }\n886:       });\n887:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n888: }\n889: \n890: uint64_t ListenerManagerImpl::numConnections() const {\n891:   uint64_t num_connections = 0;\n892:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n893:     num_connections += worker->numConnections();\n894:   }\n895: \n896:   return num_connections;\n897: }\n898: \n899: bool ListenerManagerImpl::removeListener(const std::string& name) {\n900:   return removeListenerInternal(name, true);\n901: }\n902: \n903: bool ListenerManagerImpl::removeListenerInternal(const std::string& name,\n904:                                                  bool dynamic_listeners_only) {\n905:   ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"begin remove listener: name={}\", name);\n906: \n907:   auto existing_active_listener = getListenerByName(active_listeners_, name);\n908:   auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, name);\n909:   if ((existing_warming_listener == warming_listeners_.end() ||\n910:        (dynamic_listeners_only && (*existing_warming_listener)->blockRemove())) &&\n911:       (existing_active_listener == active_listeners_.end() ||\n912:        (dynamic_listeners_only && (*existing_active_listener)->blockRemove()))) {\n913:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"unknown/locked listener '{}'. no remove\", name);\n914:     return false;\n915:   }\n916: \n917:   // Destroy a warming listener directly.\n918:   if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end()) {\n919:     (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"removing warming listener\");\n920:     warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n921:   }\n922: \n923:   // If there is an active listener it needs to be moved to draining after workers have started, or\n924:   // destroyed directly.\n925:   if (existing_active_listener != active_listeners_.end()) {\n926:     // Listeners in active_listeners_ are added to workers after workers start, so we drain\n927:     // listeners only after this occurs.\n928:     // Finish active_listeners_ transformation before calling `drainListener` as it depends on their\n929:     // state.\n930:     auto listener = std::move(*existing_active_listener);\n931:     active_listeners_.erase(existing_active_listener);\n932:     if (workers_started_) {\n933:       drainListener(std::move(listener));\n934:     }\n935:   }\n936: \n937:   stats_.listener_removed_.inc();\n938:   updateWarmingActiveGauges();\n939:   return true;\n940: }\n941: \n942: absl::Status ListenerManagerImpl::startWorkers(OptRef<GuardDog> guard_dog,\n943:                                                std::function<void()> callback) {\n944:   ENVOY_LOG(info, \"all dependencies initialized. starting workers\");\n945:   ASSERT(!workers_started_);\n946:   workers_started_ = true;\n947:   uint32_t i = 0;\n948: \n949:   absl::BlockingCounter workers_waiting_to_run(workers_.size());\n950:   std::function<void()> worker_started_running = [&workers_waiting_to_run]() {\n951:     workers_waiting_to_run.DecrementCount();\n952:   };\n953: \n954:   // We can not use \"Cleanup\" to simplify this logic here, because it results in a issue if Envoy is\n955:   // killed before workers are actually started. Specifically the AdminRequestGetStatsAndKill test\n956:   // case in main_common_test fails with ASAN error if we use \"Cleanup\" here.\n957:   const auto listeners_pending_init =\n958:       std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size() * active_listeners_.size());\n959:   ASSERT(warming_listeners_.empty());\n960:   // We need to protect against inline deletion so have to use iterators directly.\n961:   for (auto listener_it = active_listeners_.begin(); listener_it != active_listeners_.end();) {\n962:     auto& listener = *listener_it;\n963:     listener_it++;\n964: \n965:     absl::StatusOr<bool> init_status = doFinalPreWorkerListenerInit(*listener);\n966:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK_REF(init_status.status());\n967: \n968:     if (!*init_status) {\n969:       incListenerCreateFailureStat();\n970:       removeListenerInternal(listener->name(), false);\n971:       continue;\n972:     }\n973:     for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n974:       addListenerToWorker(*worker, absl::nullopt, *listener,\n975:                           [this, listeners_pending_init, callback]() {\n976:                             if (--(*listeners_pending_init) == 0) {\n977:                               stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\n978:                               callback();\n979:                             }\n980:                           });\n981:     }\n982:   }\n983:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n984:     ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"starting worker {}\", i);\n985:     worker->start(guard_dog, worker_started_running);\n986:     if (enable_dispatcher_stats_) {\n987:       worker->initializeStats(*scope_);\n988:     }\n989:     i++;\n990:   }\n991: \n992:   // Wait for workers to start running.\n993:   workers_waiting_to_run.Wait();\n994: \n995:   if (active_listeners_.empty()) {\n996:     stats_.workers_started_.set(1);\n997:     callback();\n998:   }\n999:   return absl::OkStatus();\n1000: }\n1001: \n1002: void ListenerManagerImpl::stopListener(Network::ListenerConfig& listener,\n1003:                                        const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options,\n1004:                                        std::function<void()> callback) {\n1005:   const auto workers_pending_stop = std::make_shared<std::atomic<uint64_t>>(workers_.size());\n1006:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n1007:     worker->stopListener(listener, options, [this, callback, workers_pending_stop]() {\n1008:       if (--(*workers_pending_stop) == 0) {\n1009:         server_.dispatcher().post(callback);\n1010:       }\n1011:     });\n1012:   }\n1013: }\n1014: \n1015: void ListenerManagerImpl::stopListeners(StopListenersType stop_listeners_type,\n1016:                                         const Network::ExtraShutdownListenerOptions& options) {\n1017:   stop_listeners_type_ = stop_listeners_type;\n1018:   for (Network::ListenerConfig& listener : listeners()) {\n1019:     if (stop_listeners_type != StopListenersType::InboundOnly ||\n1020:         listener.listenerInfo()->direction() == envoy::config::core::v3::INBOUND) {\n1021:       ENVOY_LOG(debug, \"begin stop listener: name={}\", listener.name());\n1022:       auto existing_warming_listener = getListenerByName(warming_listeners_, listener.name());\n1023:       // Destroy a warming listener directly.\n1024:       if (existing_warming_listener != warming_listeners_.end()) {\n1025:         (*existing_warming_listener)->debugLog(\"removing warming listener\");\n1026:         warming_listeners_.erase(existing_warming_listener);\n1027:       }\n1028:       // Close the socket once all workers stopped accepting its connections.\n1029:       // This allows clients to fast fail instead of waiting in the accept queue.\n1030:       const uint64_t listener_tag = listener.listenerTag();\n1031:       stopListener(listener, options, [this, listener_tag]() {\n1032:         stats_.listener_stopped_.inc();\n1033:         for (auto& listener : active_listeners_) {\n1034:           if (listener->listenerTag() == listener_tag) {\n1035:             maybeCloseSocketsForListener(*listener);\n1036:           }\n1037:         }\n1038:       });\n1039:     }\n1040:   }\n1041: }\n1042: \n1043: void ListenerManagerImpl::stopWorkers() {\n1044:   if (!workers_started_) {\n1045:     return;\n1046:   }\n1047:   for (const auto& worker : workers_) {\n1048:     worker->stop();\n1049:   }\n1050: }\n1051: \n1052: void ListenerManagerImpl::endListenerUpdate(FailureStates&& failure_states) {\n1053:   overall_error_state_ = std::move(failure_states);\n1054: }\n1055: \n1056: ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder::ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder(\n1057:     ListenerImpl& listener,\n1058:     Server::Configuration::TransportSocketFactoryContextImpl& factory_context)\n1059:     : listener_(listener), validator_(listener.validation_visitor_),\n1060:       listener_component_factory_(*listener.parent_.factory_), factory_context_(factory_context) {}\n1061: \n1062: absl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr>\n1063: ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder::buildFilterChain(\n1064:     const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\n1065:     FilterChainFactoryContextCreator& context_creator) const {\n1066:   return buildFilterChainInternal(filter_chain,\n1067:                                   context_creator.createFilterChainFactoryContext(&filter_chain));\n1068: }\n1069: \n1070: absl::StatusOr<Network::DrainableFilterChainSharedPtr>\n1071: ListenerFilterChainFactoryBuilder::buildFilterChainInternal(\n1072:     const envoy::config::listener::v3::FilterChain& filter_chain,\n1073:     Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContextPtr&& filter_chain_factory_context) const {\n1074:   // If the cluster doesn't have transport socket configured, then use the default \"raw_buffer\"\n1075:   // transport socket or BoringSSL-based \"tls\" transport socket if TLS settings are configured.\n1076:   // We copy by value first then override if necessary.\n1077:   auto transport_socket = filter_chain.transport_socket();\n1078:   if (!filter_chain.has_transport_socket()) {\n1079:     envoy::extensions::transport_sockets::raw_buffer::v3::RawBuffer raw_buffer;\n1080:     transport_socket.mutable_typed_config()->PackFrom(raw_buffer);\n1081:     transport_socket.set_name(\"envoy.transport_sockets.raw_buffer\");\n1082:   }\n1083: \n1084:   auto& config_factory = Config::Utility::getAndCheckFactory<\n1085:       Server::Configuration::DownstreamTransportSocketConfigFactory>(transport_socket);\n1086:   // The only connection oriented UDP transport protocol right now is QUIC.\n1087:   const bool is_quic =\n1088:       listener_.udpListenerConfig().has_value() &&\n1089:       !listener_.udpListenerConfig()->listenerFactory().isTransportConnectionless();\n1090: #if defined(ENVOY_ENABLE_QUIC)\n1091:   if (is_quic &&\n1092:       dynamic_cast<Quic::QuicServerTransportSocketConfigFactory*>(&config_factory) == nullptr) {\n1093:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n1094:         fmt::format(\"error building filter chain for quic listener: wrong \"\n1095:                     \"transport socket config specified for quic transport socket: \"\n1096:                     \"{}. \\nUse QuicDownstreamTransport instead.\",\n1097:                     transport_socket.DebugString()));\n1098:   }\n1099:   const std::string hcm_str =\n1100:       \"type.googleapis.com/\"\n1101:       \"envoy.extensions.filters.network.http_connection_manager.v3.HttpConnectionManager\";\n1102:   if (is_quic &&\n1103:       (filter_chain.filters().empty() ||\n1104:        filter_chain.filters(filter_chain.filters().size() - 1).typed_config().type_url() !=\n1105:            hcm_str)) {\n1106:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n1107:         fmt::format(\"error building network filter chain for quic listener: requires \"\n1108:                     \"http_connection_manager filter to be last in the chain.\"));\n1109:   }\n1110: #else\n1111:   // When QUIC is compiled out it should not be possible to configure either the QUIC transport\n1112:   // socket or the QUIC listener and get to this point.\n1113:   ASSERT(!is_quic);\n1114: #endif\n1115:   ProtobufTypes::MessagePtr message =\n1116:       Config::Utility::translateToFactoryConfig(transport_socket, validator_, config_factory);\n1117: \n1118:   std::vector<std::string> server_names(filter_chain.filter_chain_match().server_names().begin(),\n1119:                                         filter_chain.filter_chain_match().server_names().end());\n1120: \n1121:   auto factory_or_error = config_factory.createTransportSocketFactory(*message, factory_context_,\n1122:                                                                       std::move(server_names));\n1123:   RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(factory_or_error.status());\n1124:   auto factory_list_or_error = listener_component_factory_.createNetworkFilterFactoryList(\n1125:       filter_chain.filters(), *filter_chain_factory_context);\n1126:   RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(factory_list_or_error.status());\n1127: \n1128:   auto filter_chain_res = std::make_shared<FilterChainImpl>(\n1129:       std::move(factory_or_error.value()), std::move(*factory_list_or_error),\n1130:       std::chrono::milliseconds(\n1131:           PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(filter_chain, transport_socket_connect_timeout, 0)),\n1132:       filter_chain.name());\n1133: \n1134:   filter_chain_res->setFilterChainFactoryContext(std::move(filter_chain_factory_context));\n1135:   return filter_chain_res;\n1136: }\n1137: \n1138: absl::Status ListenerManagerImpl::setNewOrDrainingSocketFactory(const std::string& name,\n1139:                                                                 ListenerImpl& listener) {\n1140:   if (hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(warming_listeners_, listener) ||\n1141:       hasListenerWithDuplicatedAddress(active_listeners_, listener)) {\n1142:     const std::string message =\n1143:         fmt::format(\"error adding listener: '{}' has duplicate address '{}' as existing listener\",\n1144:                     name, absl::StrJoin(listener.addresses(), \",\", Network::AddressStrFormatter()));\n1145:     ENVOY_LOG(warn, \"{}\", message);\n1146:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(message);\n1147:   }\n1148: \n1149:   // Search through draining listeners to see if there is a listener that has a socket factory for\n1150:   // the same address we are configured for. This is an edge case, but\n1151:   // may happen if a listener is removed and then added back with a same or different name and\n1152:   // intended to listen on the same address. This should work and not fail.\n1153:   const ListenerImpl* draining_listener_ptr = nullptr;\n1154:   auto existing_draining_listener =\n1155:       std::find_if(draining_listeners_.cbegin(), draining_listeners_.cend(),\n1156:                    [&listener](const DrainingListener& draining_listener) {\n1157:                      return draining_listener.listener_->listenSocketFactories()[0]\n1158:                                 ->getListenSocket(0)\n1159:                                 ->isOpen() &&\n1160:                             listener.hasCompatibleAddress(*draining_listener.listener_);\n1161:                    });\n1162: \n1163:   if (existing_draining_listener != draining_listeners_.cend()) {\n1164:     existing_draining_listener->listener_->debugLog(\"clones listener sockets\");\n1165:     draining_listener_ptr = existing_draining_listener->listener_.get();\n1166:   } else {\n1167:     auto existing_draining_filter_chain = std::find_if(\n1168:         draining_filter_chains_manager_.cbegin(), draining_filter_chains_manager_.cend(),\n1169:         [&listener](const DrainingFilterChainsManager& draining_filter_chain) {\n1170:           return draining_filter_chain.getDrainingListener()\n1171:                      .listenSocketFactories()[0]\n1172:                      ->getListenSocket(0)\n1173:                      ->isOpen() &&\n1174:                  listener.hasCompatibleAddress(draining_filter_chain.getDrainingListener());\n1175:         });\n1176: \n1177:     if (existing_draining_filter_chain != draining_filter_chains_manager_.cend()) {\n1178:       existing_draining_filter_chain->getDrainingListener().debugLog(\"clones listener socket\");\n1179:       draining_listener_ptr = &existing_draining_filter_chain->getDrainingListener();\n1180:     }\n1181:   }\n1182: \n1183:   if (draining_listener_ptr != nullptr) {\n1184:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(listener.cloneSocketFactoryFrom(*draining_listener_ptr));\n1185:   } else {\n1186:     return createListenSocketFactory(listener);\n1187:   }\n1188:   return absl::OkStatus();\n1189: }\n1190: \n1191: absl::Status ListenerManagerImpl::createListenSocketFactory(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n1192:   Network::Socket::Type socket_type = listener.socketType();\n1193:   ListenerComponentFactory::BindType bind_type = ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::NoBind;\n1194:   if (listener.bindToPort()) {\n1195:     bind_type = listener.reusePort() ? ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::ReusePort\n1196:                                      : ListenerComponentFactory::BindType::NoReusePort;\n1197:   }\n1198:   absl::Status socket_status = absl::OkStatus();\n1199:   TRY_ASSERT_MAIN_THREAD {\n1200:     Network::SocketCreationOptions creation_options;\n1201:     creation_options.mptcp_enabled_ = listener.mptcpEnabled();\n1202:     for (std::vector<Network::Address::InstanceConstSharedPtr>::size_type i = 0;\n1203:          i < listener.addresses().size(); i++) {\n1204:       auto factory_or_error = ListenSocketFactoryImpl::create(\n1205:           *factory_, listener.addresses()[i], socket_type, listener.listenSocketOptions(i),\n1206:           listener.name(), listener.tcpBacklogSize(), bind_type, creation_options,\n1207:           server_.options().concurrency());\n1208:       if (!factory_or_error.status().ok()) {\n1209:         socket_status = factory_or_error.status();\n1210:       } else {\n1211:         socket_status = listener.addSocketFactory(std::move(*factory_or_error));\n1212:       }\n1213:       if (!socket_status.ok()) {\n1214:         break;\n1215:       }\n1216:     }\n1217:   }\n1218:   END_TRY\n1219:   CATCH(const EnvoyException& e, {\n1220:     socket_status = absl::InvalidArgumentError(e.what());\n1221:     ;\n1222:   });\n1223:   if (!socket_status.ok()) {\n1224:     ENVOY_LOG(error, \"listener '{}' failed to bind or apply socket options: {}\", listener.name(),\n1225:               socket_status.message());\n1226:     incListenerCreateFailureStat();\n1227:   }\n1228:   return socket_status;\n1229: }\n1230: \n1231: void ListenerManagerImpl::maybeCloseSocketsForListener(ListenerImpl& listener) {\n1232:   if (!listener.udpListenerConfig().has_value() ||\n1233:       listener.udpListenerConfig()->listenerFactory().isTransportConnectionless()) {\n1234:     // Close the listen sockets right away to avoid leaving TCP connections in accept queue\n1235:     // already waiting for long timeout. However, connection-oriented UDP listeners shouldn't\n1236:     // close the socket because they need to receive packets for existing connections via the\n1237:     // listen sockets.\n1238:     listener.closeAllSockets();\n1239: \n1240:     // In case of this listener was in-place updated previously and in the filter chains draining\n1241:     // procedure, so close the sockets for the previous draining listener.\n1242:     for (auto& manager : draining_filter_chains_manager_) {\n1243:       // A listener can be in-place updated multiple times, so there may\n1244:       // have multiple draining listeners with same tag.\n1245:       if (manager.getDrainingListenerTag() == listener.listenerTag()) {\n1246:         manager.getDrainingListener().closeAllSockets();\n1247:       }\n1248:     }\n1249:   }\n1250: }\n1251: \n1252: ApiListenerOptRef ListenerManagerImpl::apiListener() {\n1253:   return api_listener_ ? ApiListenerOptRef(std::ref(*api_listener_)) : absl::nullopt;\n1254: }\n1255: \n1256: REGISTER_FACTORY(DefaultListenerManagerFactoryImpl, ListenerManagerFactory);\n1257: \n1258: } // namespace Server\n1259: } // namespace Envoy\n1260: ",

The filter factory list is created from\\n42:  * statically registered filters.\\n43:  */\\n44: class ProdListenerComponentFactory : public ListenerComponentFactory,\\n45:                                      Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\\n46: public:\\n47:   ProdListenerComponentFactory(Instance& server) : server_(server) {}\\n48:   /**\\n49:    * Static worker for createNetworkFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\\n50:    */\\n51:   static absl::StatusOr<Filter::NetworkFilterFactoriesList> createNetworkFilterFactoryListImpl(\\n52:       const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::Filter>& filters,\\n53:       Configuration::FilterChainFactoryContext& filter_chain_factory_context,\\n54:       Filter::NetworkFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl& config_provider_manager);\\n55: \\n56:   /**\\n57:    * Static worker for createListenerFilterFactoryList() that can be used directly in tests.\\n58:    */\\n59:   static absl::StatusOr<Filter::ListenerFilterFactoriesList> createListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(\\n60:       const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\\n61:       Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context,\\n62:       Filter::TcpListenerFilterConfigProviderManagerImpl& config_provider_manager);\\n63: \\n64:   /**\\n65:    * Static lds_resources_locator) override {\\n87:     return std::make_unique<LdsApiImpl>(\\n88:         lds_config, lds_resources_locator, server_.clusterManager(), server_.initManager(),\\n89:         *server_.stats().rootScope(), server_.listenerManager(),\\n90:         server_.messageValidationContext().dynamicValidationVisitor());\\n91:   }\\n92:                                  network_config_provider_manager_);\\n97:   }\\n98:   absl::StatusOr<Filter::ListenerFilterFactoriesList> createListenerFilterFactoryList(\\n99:       const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\\n100:       Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context) override {\\n101:     return createListenerFilterFactoryListImpl(filters, context,\\n102:                                                tcp_listener_config_provider_manager_);\\n103:   }\\n104:   absl::StatusOr<std::vector<Network::UdpListenerFilterFactoryCb>>\\n105:   createUdpListenerFilterFactoryList(\\n106:       const Protobuf::RepeatedPtrField<envoy::config::listener::v3::ListenerFilter>& filters,\\n107:       Configuration::ListenerFactoryContext& context) override {\\n108:     return
